prompts.text: 15-second, no-dialogue, fully immersive high-speed brutal fight short film. Two characters @ image1 and @ image2 . Live-action realistic style. Close-quarters, high-speed chaotic melee combat with no rules, fully out-of-control brawling. No flashy choreography—pure raw. Continuous exchange of punches, kicks, blocks, body checks, throws, and grappling entanglements. Ultra-realistic impact. Exaggerated yet physically grounded motion speed. Entire sequence in 120 FPS with no slow motion. Rapid dashes, evasions, and high-speed limb swings. Extreme motion blur and speed trails amplify intensity while preserving realistic weight and physics. Master-level cinematic camera movement: A professional film camera stays tightly locked to the action, flying dynamically with the fighters—high-speed dives, sudden stops, whip pans, and full 360° orbital tracking. The camera moves in sync with punches, kicks, and evasions, with responsive shake and displacement. One continuous take, no cuts. Sharp push-pull tracking, sudden directional shifts, fully integrated into the fight for a face-to-face immersive combat perspective. Smooth, zero-latency motion. Master-level precision editing: 0–3 seconds: ultra-fast cuts to enter combat. 3–10 seconds: seamless action continuity with zero stutter or breaks. 10–15 seconds: dense, violent rapid-cut climax. Editing rhythm escalates with combat intensity. Fast intercutting enhances chaos. Precise beat timing, no redundant shots. Constant suffocating high-speed pacing. Environment Setting Modern open-plan office with desks, computers, cabinets, printers, and swivel chairs; realistic layout with cool overhead lighting and side backlight for strong contrast. Slightly cramped space for added tension; no glass-breaking. Environmental Interaction & Constraints: Combat affects the space only through natural, physically justified collisions—papers scatter from air movement (not attacks), chairs slide or topple on impact, desks shift slightly without deliberate damage, and screens remain intact with minor vibration. No unnecessary or intentional destruction of equipment. Body-driven combat: grappling, pinning, counter-throws, and realistic slams with subtle impact cracks/vibrations. Natural airflow—light paper/dust movement only, no exaggeration. Final Action Design Final 2–3s: both throw simultaneous close-range punches, freezing inches before impact—fully tensed, heavy breathing, locked in perfect deadlock. #OpenArt #SeedanceShowdown #TeamOpenArtist

No unnecessary or intentional destruction of equipment. Body-driven combat: grappling, pinning, counter-throws, and realistic slams with subtle impact cracks/vibrations. Natural airflow—light paper/dust movement only, no exaggeration. Final Action Design Final 2–3s: both throw simultaneous close-range punches, freezing inches before impact—fully tensed, heavy breathing, locked in perfect deadlock.
prompts.author: @doctorwasif
prompts.tweet_text:
Even the most extraordinary action scene you can imagine can become reality with a single prompt.

Seedance 2 on @openart_ai 

Prompt:
15-second, no-dialogue, fully immersive high-speed brutal fight short film.

Two characters @ image1 and @ image2 . 
Live-action realistic style. Close-quarters, high-speed chaotic melee combat with no rules, fully out-of-control brawling. No flashy choreography—pure raw. Continuous exchange of punches, kicks, blocks, body checks, throws, and grappling entanglements. Ultra-realistic impact. Exaggerated yet physically grounded motion speed. Entire sequence in 120 FPS with no slow motion. Rapid dashes, evasions, and high-speed limb swings. Extreme motion blur and speed trails amplify intensity while preserving realistic weight and physics.

Master-level cinematic camera movement:

A professional film camera stays tightly locked to the action, flying dynamically with the fighters—high-speed dives, sudden stops, whip pans, and full 360° orbital tracking. The camera moves in sync with punches, kicks, and evasions, with responsive shake and displacement. One continuous take, no cuts. Sharp push-pull tracking, sudden directional shifts, fully integrated into the fight for a face-to-face immersive combat perspective. Smooth, zero-latency motion.

Master-level precision editing:

0–3 seconds: ultra-fast cuts to enter combat.

3–10 seconds: seamless action continuity with zero stutter or breaks.

10–15 seconds: dense, violent rapid-cut climax.

Editing rhythm escalates with combat intensity. Fast intercutting enhances chaos. Precise beat timing, no redundant shots. Constant suffocating high-speed pacing.

Environment Setting

Modern open-plan office with desks, computers, cabinets, printers, and swivel chairs; realistic layout with cool overhead lighting and side backlight for strong contrast. Slightly cramped space for added tension; no glass-breaking.

Environmental Interaction & Constraints:

Combat affects the space only through natural, physically justified collisions—papers scatter from air movement (not attacks), chairs slide or topple on impact, desks shift slightly without deliberate damage, and screens remain intact with minor vibration. No unnecessary or intentional destruction of equipment.

Body-driven combat: grappling, pinning, counter-throws, and realistic slams with subtle impact cracks/vibrations. Natural airflow—light paper/dust movement only, no exaggeration.

Final Action Design

Final 2–3s: both throw simultaneous close-range punches, freezing inches before impact—fully tensed, heavy breathing, locked in perfect deadlock.

prompts.text: Intro scene: Close up of a bank window from the scene the outside at night, red and blue police lights flashing across the glass surface. The camera performs a subtle push in. Suddenly the small white bunny in the oversized leather jacket EXPLODES through the window from inside, shattering the glass outward, screaming with his mouth wide open. He is clutching an oversized duffle bag stuffed with visible dollar bills spilling out, the bag is almost bigger than he is. Glass shards fly everywhere. He bursts through into open air and for a moment he hangs there suspended — his tiny legs running frantically in mid-air like a classic cartoon character who hasn't realized there's no ground yet, paws cycling fast going nowhere. A beat of suspension. Then gravity wins and he drops out of frame downward. The camera stays locked on the broken window, not following him. We see the jagged hole in the glass, smoke drifting out, red emergency light pulsing from inside the bank, a few last dollar bills fluttering out through the broken window. Hold on the empty broken window. No music. Sound effects and foley only: glass explosion, Cuco's scream, frantic paw movement sounds, wind, the thud of landing below frame, dollar bills fluttering, distant sirens and helicopter from the previous scene still audible.

prompts.text: *“Anime cooking scene, Makoto Shinkai cinematic style, side-angle camera. Flour-dusted wooden table, sunlight streaming through window, all ingredients arranged in bowls: tomato sauce, cherry tomatoes, salami, mushrooms, peppers, shredded mozzarella, parmesan, fresh basil, olive oil bottle, dried oregano, red pepper flakes. Hands pick up dough ball, begin stretching it sideways — dough pulls and stretches thin, flour dust lifts into sunlight in slow motion. Hands place stretched dough flat on floured surface, grab wooden rolling pin — roll firmly forward and back, dough spreads wide, edges curl slightly. Hands lift dough round, lay it flat. Spoon scoops tomato sauce from red bowl, pours onto center of dough — back of spoon spreads it in slow confident spiral outward, sauce glistens. Hands drizzle olive oil bottle in thin stream over sauce — golden drops catch sunlight. Right hand grabs shredded mozzarella by the handful, scatters generously — cheese lands softly across surface. Hands layer toppings one by one: salami slices placed in overlapping circle, mushroom slices dropped casually, colorful pepper pieces scattered, cherry tomatoes halved and pressed gently into cheese. Fingers pinch dried oregano from jar, crumble over pizza — fine herb dust floats in light rays. Pinch of red pepper flakes scattered. Fresh basil leaves torn by hand, placed last. Hands slide pizza onto wooden peel. Camera pulls back — full pizza visible, golden afternoon light, flour particles glowing, volumetric light rays through window. Rich layered sounds throughout: dough stretching, rolling pin on wood, sauce spreading, cheese scattering, toppings dropping. 15 seconds, smooth flowing pacing, ultra-detailed food animation.”* Step 5: Click Generate and wait for the result

prompts.author: @1littlecoder
prompts.text: The camera rockets up from below, tearing past jagged cliff walls, then whips into a downward tilt to reveal the ocean below in full violent eruption, waves detonating against black rock. On the cliff edge stands a warrior woman, utterly fearless, gazing into the maelstrom as if daring it to rise higher. She steps forward without hesitation and leaps off the edge, arms spread wide, plummeting through howling wind and salt spray. The camera dives alongside her in freefall, her hair and cloak thrashing upward as the black rocks and churning sea rush closer. Then a massive shadow tears through the mist below — a dragon surges upward with wings unfurling like storm sails, scales gleaming wet with ocean spray. It swoops beneath her mid-fall, and she lands on its back with practiced grace, fingers gripping the ridges along its neck. The dragon banks hard, climbing in a spiraling arc back up past the cliff face. The camera keeps pace, circling tight, then pulls ahead into a final close, frontal reveal: she rides the beast skyward, wind-whipped, calm, and burning with determination, the raging ocean now small and powerless far below.

prompts.text: Seedance 2.0 Ai Video Prompt used ⏬ Start inside the mouth of a colossal underground cavern as the camera rushes forward and drops in a spiraling dive into the depths. Below, a mag-lev racing pod hurtles along a natural crystal rail formation deep underground, banking through impossible geological curves. Its design is minimal and luminous: a teardrop-shaped transparent cockpit revealing the pilot within, magnetic suspension rings that pulse electric blue, no visible propulsion — just pure electromagnetic force, and a surface so polished it mirrors the crystal walls rushing past. Giant amethyst and quartz formations blur by in streaks of purple and white while the pod's magnetic field makes the crystals around it resonate and sing. Without a cut, the camera tightens from the cavern-wide dive into a close chase along the crystal rail, revealing the pod's reflection fractured across a thousand crystal facets, electrical arcs jumping between the magnetic rings and nearby mineral deposits, and the crystal formations vibrating visibly, emitting harmonic tones. It drops below the rail for a dramatic undercarriage shot revealing the magnetic suspension gap — a thin line of pure blue-white energy — then whips to the front as the cavern opens into a massive geode chamber. For the climax, the crystal rail splits into three diverging paths around a central formation — an enormous singular crystal, twenty meters tall, glowing from within with trapped ancient light. The pod accelerates toward the fork, then at the last instant detaches from the rail entirely, going airborne through the geode chamber. It clips the giant crystal as it passes — the crystal shatters in slow motion, releasing its trapped light in a blinding prismatic explosion that fills the cavern with rainbow fire. The pod rides the light-blast's shockwave upward through a vertical shaft and erupts from a mountainside into daylight. The camera spirals outward for one final epic reveal — the mountain exhaling a column of rainbow light into the sky.

prompts.text: Prompt used Start high above a dense Amazonian rainforest canopy, an unbroken green ocean, as the camera drops in a vertical plunge through a gap in the trees. Below the canopy, a compact wasp-like reconnaissance drone tears through the mid-story at terrifying speed, dodging trunks and vines. Its design is insectoid and aggressive: iridescent dark green carapace, four articulated rotor-wings that fold and extend independently for impossible maneuvers, compound-lens camera eyes that glow amber, and a rear stinger antenna crackling with scanning pulses. Parrots explode from branches, leaves shred in its rotor wash, and spider webs snap like glass. Without a cut, the camera follows from wide canopy breach into an intimate chase through the green cathedral, revealing individual leaves slicing off vine stems, moisture misting off the rotors in spiral patterns, bark fragments spraying from near-miss tree trunks, and shafts of dappled sunlight strobing across the carapace. It darts ahead through a curtain of hanging moss for a dramatic reveal shot as the drone bursts through behind it, then spirals around a massive trunk alongside the drone in a synchronized helix. For the climax, the canopy ahead is choked by an enormous fallen tree draped in vines — a solid wall of vegetation. The drone folds all four rotor-wings flat against its body, becoming a dart, and fires its scanning pulse forward — the pulse illuminates a narrow gap in the debris. The drone threads the gap in a spinning corkscrew, vines whipping off its folded wings, and explodes out the other side into a hidden clearing where a massive waterfall cascades into a crystal pool. The camera spirals upward through the mist and rainbow spray for one final epic reveal — the secret paradise hidden within the endless green.

prompts.text: Check it here http:// socialsight.ai Prompt A stylish young man walks confidently through a busy urban street, wearing a brand-new, high-end football jersey paired with sleek sneakers and perfectly matching trousers. The environment is lively—people walking, cars moving, city sounds all around. He suddenly stops, raises his hands, and claps once. Instantly, everything around him freezes in time—people mid-step, cars halted, birds suspended in the air. The world becomes completely still, except for him. A smile spreads across his face. He begins to dance freely, full of joy and energy, celebrating in the frozen world. He spins, grooves, and runs playfully through the motionless crowd, expressing pure happiness. After a while, he pauses, looks around, and claps his hands again. In that moment, everything comes back to life—movement resumes naturally as if nothing happened. The man casually continues walking forward with a satisfied smile, blending back into the crowd. Style: cinematic, slow motion transitions, vibrant colors, high detail, realistic lighting, dynamic camera angles.

prompts.text: I have used Seedance 2 for a few days now and here is my personal take- It’s not as useful as it appears and @Kling_ai is still by far the cinematic storytellers best tool. Kling is collaborative and good at taking direction and interpreting it to meet my intent. Seedance is good at executing a detailed blueprint layout with cold precision and shine. That difference is crystal clear to me, with a storyteller first mindset. Seedance interprets blueprints expertly and Kling responds to direction like a good DP and camera man. A blueprint approach says: here are the parts, here is the continuity, here is the construction. A Direction approach says: here is the intention, here is the emphasis, here is what matters emotionally, here is how the audience should feel as this unfolds. For me, Seedance becomes B roll and fast action scenes between dramatic moments.

prompts.text: "Animate a short video where a central character stands calmly. A single armored knight approaches the character, holding a golden crown. As the knight reaches the character, a second armored knight appears and gently places their hands on the crown from above, guiding it onto the character's head. Ensure the crown is positioned correctly, without clipping or intersecting the characters' bodies. Add subtle dynamic motion: armor glinting, slow flowing capes, hair and clothing of the main character reacting naturally, soft ambient lighting. Smooth cinematic camera follow, gentle idle motion after the crowning. Emphasize grandeur and regal atmosphere, photorealistic or stylized cinematic look, precise positioning, collision-free animation."

prompts.text: FROM SEED TO SLICE — The Life of Food Cinematic continuous side-scrolling sequence, 16:9, 15 seconds. One unbroken shot — the camera tracks left to right following a single tomato from seed to pizza slice, through every step of the food chain. [0:00–0:02] A farmer's weathered hand pushes a tiny seed into rich Italian soil. Rain falls. A sprout pushes through. Fast-forward: the vine grows, twists, flowers bloom, and a perfect red tomato swells into frame — glistening, sun-warmed, impossibly beautiful. The farmer picks it with a gentle twist. [0:02–0:04] The field becomes a bustling Italian market. The tomato rolls across a wooden market stall among hundreds of others. Hands sort, weigh, bag. An old woman squeezes it gently, nods approvingly, drops coins on the counter. The tomato rides in her basket through cobblestone streets. [0:04–0:06] The street becomes a cramped, steaming kitchen. The tomato is washed under running water, placed on a worn cutting board. A chef's knife comes down — SLOW MOTION — the tomato splits open, seeds and juice catching the light like liquid rubies. Crushed by hand into a copper pot. Garlic sizzles. Basil tears. Steam rises. [0:06–0:08] The sauce bubbles. Flour erupts as dough is punched, stretched, spun in the air — it becomes a perfect pizza base. The sauce is ladled in spirals. Mozzarella stretches and tears in beautiful white ribbons. Into a wood-fired oven — flames lick the crust, cheese bubbles, edges char perfectly. [0:08–0:10] The oven becomes a restaurant window. The pizza slides out on a wooden paddle. A pizza cutter wheels through it — the crunch, the steam, the cheese pull. A single perfect slice is lifted — the cheese stretches in a long golden strand that finally snaps. [0:10–0:12] The slice crosses a busy restaurant. A waiter carries it through the chaos — dodging other waiters, squeezing between tables. It arrives at a small table by the window where a father and daughter sit. The little girl's eyes go wide. [0:12–0:14] She takes a massive bite. Cheese on her chin. Sauce on her cheek. Her eyes close. Pure bliss. She chews with her whole body — nodding, wiggling, kicking her feet under the table. The father watches her and smiles. He takes a bite too. [0:14–0:15] Pull out through the restaurant window. The Italian town at golden hour. Church bells. The tomato field is visible on the hill in the distance, where it all began. Fade to warm black. Sound: rain on soil/birdsong → market chatter/coins clinking → knife chop/garlic sizzle/bubbling → dough thump/fire crackle → pizza cutter crunch/cheese stretch → restaurant bustle → child's "mmmm"/giggling → church bells → silence.

[0:14–0:15] Pull out through the restaurant window. The Italian town at golden hour. Church bells. The tomato field is visible on the hill in the distance, where it all began. Fade to warm black. Sound: rain on soil/birdsong → market chatter/coins clinking → knife chop/garlic sizzle/bubbling → dough thump/fire crackle → pizza cutter crunch/cheese stretch → restaurant bustle → child's "mmmm"/giggling → church bells → silence.

prompts.text: With due respect, ma’am 🙏😿 Not the usual vibe—cat master is bad this time 😳🐱 (made with @nemovideoai Seedance 2) Prompt share 👁‍🗨👇 15-second, high-speed action-comedy short film. Dialogue-driven. Brutal combat with dark humor. STYLE: Live-action realism with stylized animal characters. Chaotic, close-quarters melee. Messy, aggressive, no-rule fighting. 120 FPS, no slow motion. Heavy motion blur with grounded impact. White Cat’s movement contrasts sharply with fluid, ballet-like precision. CHARACTERS: Orange Cat, college kid in oversized hoodie and sneakers, loud and cocky. Hyena, dangerous, wearing sunglasses and gray suit, calm but unstable. White Cat, elegant, pink dress, red heels, composed, transforms into graceful precision violence. ENVIRONMENT: NYC subway car, crowded, metallic interior, flickering lights, tight space amplifying chaos. CAMERA: Single continuous take. Aggressive tracking, whip pans, snap zooms, close face-level perspective. Camera struggles to keep up, shake synced to impacts. EDITING RHYTHM: 0–3s chaos ignition 3–10s nonstop combat 10–15s elegant violent payoff ACTION: 0–3s Subway rattling. Orange Cat Bro, take off the sunglasses indoors. You look unemployed. Hyena turns slowly Say that again. Instant explosion into violence. 3–10s Full chaos. Punches, kicks, grappling, bodies slamming into poles and doors. Metal dents. Windows crack. Handles rip off. Passengers scream and scatter OH MY GOD I JUST WANTED TO GO HOME Orange Cat gets smashed into seats, rebounds, tackles Hyena. Hyena throws him into a door, loud crack. Rapid exchanges, slipping, choking, scrambling. Environment breaks down Doors bent Windows spiderwebbed Panels warped inward 10–13s Hyena winds up a heavy punch toward White Cat, who has been quietly reading. She lowers the book slowly. White Cat, calm Seriously She stands in one smooth motion. Posture perfect, like preparing to perform. 13–15s Her movement shifts completely. She pivots lightly on one heel. A clean, extended kick lands with precision. Hyena jolts violently. She spins, dress flowing in a smooth arc. Another strike lands mid-rotation. Each motion is elegant, controlled, and devastating. She steps onto a bent seat like a stage. Pushes off. Final spinning kick. Hyena is launched through the subway window. Glass bursts outward. Silence. White Cat lands softly. Adjusts her dress. Walks back through the wreckage with slow, graceful steps. Heels clicking against twisted metal. She sits. Crosses her legs. Resumes reading the same page. Without looking up You were being annoying Orange Cat drops to his knees Ma’am... you saved my life... She flips a page. No response. Subway keeps moving. Cut. #sd2 #seedance #AICatDrama #CatsLover

prompts.text: Prompt for Seedance 2.0: FORMAT: 15s / 1 shot / live rear-follow in-camera inset SUBJECTS: One blonde female rider in a low-cut flowing white dress under a locked leather coaster harness, seen from behind in the main live frame while a small bordered rectangular top-right in-camera inset keeps her visible from the waist up from the front, with high-speed excitement in her face and hair. SCENE: Lock the main camera on a rigid rear-follow mount directly behind the coaster car and never break the shot. Capture raw live ride footage with physical camera behavior and real-time force as one uninterrupted in-camera recording. Start in immediate action in a wide open fantasy sky zone as the coaster is already racing along an exposed cliffside rail toward a giant broken stone ring suspended between floating cliffs, then slam straight into a hard open-air drop. Keep the main frame chasing the rider so rails, drops, loop, creatures, and scale read clearly from behind while her hair, shoulders, low-cut white dress, and harness snap under speed. Embed a persistent small bordered rectangular in-camera picture-in-picture feed in the top right, with a clear frame edge and hard corners, showing her face, shoulders, harness, and upper body from the front, waist up, recorded simultaneously in the same uninterrupted take and never disappearing. Keep her expression intensely excited like someone at full speed on a rollercoaster, with mouth open, breath hit by speed, eyes locked into the rush, and hair whipping violently under wind and g-force. Make the inset inherit the same impacts, vibrations, rattling, g-force jolts, and speed shake from every drop, bank, loop, corkscrew, and near miss so it feels physically tied to the same ride event, not like a separate edit. Keep one continuous looped route as the coaster tears through the drop, hard bank, full vertical loop, and corkscrew, races past floating cliffs, broken arches, giant statues, storm light, dragon shadows, pterosaur-like creatures, and horned dinosaurs far below the rails, then hits a near miss as a huge dragon crosses over the track and throws firelight across the rider and metal bars. Finish by climbing and curving back into the same open sky zone and same giant broken stone ring from the opening so the ending feels like the route is looping back to its own start point. Preserve one white wardrobe state, visible rail motion at all times, rear-follow live main frame, top-right bordered rectangular front waist-up in-camera inset throughout, matched shake in both views, no side view, no cutaway, no POV swap. MOOD: Breathless awe, danger, relentless speed. COLOR LOGIC: Naturalistic Film Print Emulation STYLE: Cinematic realism, action spectacle, epic fantasy pursuit

prompts.author: @sebatheepan
prompts.text: Full prompt used ------------------ Cinematic short film, photorealistic VFX. 15-second desert hunt at high noon. Blinding light, zero shade, heat shimmer on everything. [0–2s] Overhead drone — red desert canyon system. Sandstone towers, wind-carved arches, dry riverbeds. One figure: a woman on a sand-skiff — a flat wooden board with a triangular sail, gliding across compacted sand on bone runners. She wears wrapped linen armor, copper goggles, a weighted net coiled at her waist. She watches the sand. [2–4s] ECU the sand surface — it's BREATHING. Rippling outward from a point 200 yards ahead. The sand rises like a slow dome. The sail-woman cuts the skiff hard right. The dome ERUPTS — a sandworm. 40 feet, segmented, chitinous plates the color of rust. Its mouth is a vertical iris lined with grinding teeth like a rock crusher. Sand cascades off its body. It roars — the sound is DEEP, below human hearing, but you feel it shake the frame. [4–7s] Chase through the canyon — the skiff banks between sandstone pillars. The worm follows UNDERGROUND — you see its path as a moving ridge of sand, smashing through the earth, pillars cracking as it passes beneath their foundations. She throws the weighted net — it wraps around a sandstone tower, rope trailing to her skiff. [7–10s] The worm surfaces directly in the skiff's path. She leans the board, catches wind, and the skiff JUMPS — airborne over the worm's back. In mid-air she drives a barbed stake into the top of its head plate. Lands the skiff on the other side. Rope connects stake to the sandstone tower. [10–13s] The worm charges away — the rope goes taut against the tower. The tower HOLDS. The worm's momentum whiplashes it sideways. It crashes into a canyon wall. Sandstone debris cascades. Dust cloud erupts like a bomb. Silence inside the cloud. [13–15s] Dust settles. The worm lies against the canyon wall, breathing but pinned. She brings the skiff alongside, pulls her goggles up. Beneath the goggles — calm, focused eyes. She draws a curved knife and begins cutting a single iridescent scale from the worm's flank — the harvest. That's all she came for. One scale. The worm hisses but doesn't fight. She pats its flank once, takes the scale, pushes off. The skiff catches wind. She vanishes between the sandstone towers. Cut to black. Ultra-realistic.

prompts.text: I want to create an anime video scene in Seedance 2.0. I have as reference the uploaded images which include a character sheet of a teenage girl and another of a phoenix bird, an image of the storyboard and another of a forest setting. The scene is an action scene from an adventure series, Japanese style. Analyze the images and give me back a prompt for Seedance so it generates an animation video, using as style the artistic style of the setting and emphasizing on adhering to the shot composition of the storyboard. Test the adapted prompt options given by Gemini/ChatGPT and use the one that shows the best results. Final Prompt: Use the references with strict priority and role separation: [REF_STORYBOARD] = "Image1" primary guide for shot order, framing, timing, composition, and scene progression. Follow it strictly. [REF_GIRL_MODEL] = "Image2" identity anchor for the teenage girl. [REF_PHOENIX_MODEL] "Image 3" creature identity anchor for the phoenix. [REF_BACKGROUND] = "Image 4" environment and forest background anchor. Create a Japanese anime action scene of a teenage girl fighting two phoenix birds in a forest. Absolute priority: 1. Follow the storyboard strictly for all shots and scene progression. 2. Keep the girl design locked to [REF_GIRL_MODEL]. 3. Keep the phoenix design locked to [REF_PHOENIX_MODEL]. 4. Keep the forest and path consistent with [REF_BACKGROUND].

prompts.text: Prompt Studio: Rooftop Escape with Seedance 2.0 in @openart_ai , text to video Ultra-fast FPV shot chasing a motorcycle moving across connected rooftops at night. The rider uses ramps, maintenance paths, and realistic gaps, landing hard, causing loose rooftop elements to shift and partially collapse after impact. The camera tilts downward, revealing real drop distances between buildings. It then shifts into a side tracking angle, matching the bike mid-air with believable jump arcs. The camera pulls back into reverse tracking, the rider approaching during a jump, suspension compressing on landing. Finally, it locks into a tight follow shot, continuing across unstable but plausible structures. No impossible jumps, just precision under risk.

prompts.text: “Seedance 2.0” That Could Destroy Hollywood: Video Production Costs Drop to 1/1000th of Conventional Levels, and the People Who Will Be Sent Off《Kobayashi Hirofumi》 @akihito At the core of Seedance 2.0 lies an architecture called the “Dual-Branch Diffusion Transformer (DB-DiT).” In short, rather than creating video and audio separately and then combining them afterward, it's a system that generates them simultaneously as an integrated whole from the start. To put it in perspective, while conventional AI was like dubbing a voiceover onto a picture book after the fact, Seedance 2.0 is like doing the voice acting while drawing the pictures. As a result, dialogue matches lip movements, footsteps sync with ground contact, and fabric flutters align with whooshing wind sounds—all at a frame-by-frame level of consistency. This isn't just a subjective impression. In the independent benchmark “Artificial Analysis Video Arena,” where multiple video generation models are pitted against each other via human votes in a round-robin comparison, Seedance 2.0 has surpassed established frontrunners like Google Veo 3.1, OpenAI Sora 2, and Runway Gen-4.5 to claim the top spot. On the usability front as well, it stands apart from conventional models. Users can feed Seedance 2.0 not only text-based instructions but also up to 12 reference items in total (9 images, 3 videos, 3 audio clips) bundled together. For example, you can bundle multiple requests into a single generation prompt, like “use this photo's person's face,” “mimic the movement from this clip,” and “match the vibe of this BGM.” The output is a video up to 15 seconds long at 1080p to 2K resolution, delivered as an edited scene with multiple cuts stitched together. Background music, ambient sounds, and dialogue are generated simultaneously with the visuals. There's one more decisive difference from conventional models. Seedance 2.0 can automatically generate an “edited scene” composed of multiple shots from a single prompt. While traditional AI video outputs short, single-shot fragments, this model designs its own storyboarding (shot breakdowns) and generates a sequence of scenes including changes in perspective like close-ups, pull-backs, and overhead views. It's as if the AI is handling scripting, filming, and editing all in one go. What stunned industry insiders was another figure entirely. According to third-party measurements cited by a Chinese securities analyst, Seedance 2.0's generation usability rate (the proportion of outputs usable without revisions) exceeds 90%. Conventional AI video models hover around 20% on this metric, premised on the idea that “most generations are unusable, requiring dozens of retries.” Such a sharp rise in usability means it has crossed the line into “ready to bring straight to a commercial production site.” Read more (to comments section)

This isn't just a subjective impression. In the independent benchmark “Artificial Analysis Video Arena,” where multiple video generation models are pitted against each other via human votes in a round-robin comparison, Seedance 2.0 has surpassed established frontrunners like Google Veo 3.1, OpenAI Sora 2, and Runway Gen-4.5 to claim the top spot.
On the usability front as well, it stands apart from conventional models. Users can feed Seedance 2.0 not only text-based instructions but also up to 12 reference items in total (9 images, 3 videos, 3 audio clips) bundled together.

For example, you can bundle multiple requests into a single generation prompt, like “use this photo's person's face,” “mimic the movement from this clip,” and “match the vibe of this BGM.” The output is a video up to 15 seconds long at 1080p to 2K resolution, delivered as an edited scene with multiple cuts stitched together. Background music, ambient sounds, and dialogue are generated simultaneously with the visuals.

There's one more decisive difference from conventional models. Seedance 2.0 can automatically generate an “edited scene” composed of multiple shots from a single prompt. While traditional AI video outputs short, single-shot fragments, this model designs its own storyboarding (shot breakdowns) and generates a sequence of scenes including changes in perspective like close-ups, pull-backs, and overhead views. It's as if the AI is handling scripting, filming, and editing all in one go.
What stunned industry insiders was another figure entirely. According to third-party measurements cited by a Chinese securities analyst, Seedance 2.0's generation usability rate (the proportion of outputs usable without revisions) exceeds 90%.

Conventional AI video models hover around 20% on this metric, premised on the idea that “most generations are unusable, requiring dozens of retries.” Such a sharp rise in usability means it has crossed the line into “ready to bring straight to a commercial production site.” Read more (to comments section)

prompts.text: A cinematic one-shot begins inside a silent grand museum at night. The camera moves toward a massive painting of a storm at sea. As it nears the canvas, the painted waves begin moving, and the frame transforms continuously without cuts: the painted ocean becomes real water flooding the gallery floor, the gallery becomes a ship deck in a hurricane, the sailcloth becomes desert tents, the tents become war banners on a battlefield, the banners become laundry lines in a sunlit village, the village walls become canyon cliffs, the cliffs become bookshelves in an endless library. Every transition must come from shared shapes and motion. A museum guard walks through all of it in disbelief, but the camera never leaves him. Frames become windows, windows become doorways, doorways become portals into new realities. Final transformation: the guard realizes he is now inside a painting, frozen in brushstroke texture, while museum visitors admire him from outside.

prompts.text: The cat from the image is on the very back layer, repeatedly swinging a sword in time with the sound. Completely ignore the background from the reference image. Ultra-high-speed kinetic typography (full 15 seconds), futuristic minimalist motion graphics, high-end After Effects style. Color palette consists of just three colors: black, white, and red. Extremely high-tempo staccato rhythm, precisely controlled motion, speed-filled expression, refined digital energy. 0–3 seconds: Introduction. Fragments of 'cat samurai' are scattered as ultra-fine lines and geometric markers, repeatedly appearing and disappearing amid ultra-high-speed glitch flashes. Blackwhite backgrounds switch frame by frame, with red lines running like coordinate guides. Scan lines, noise flicker, offset text afterimages, and instantaneous split slices express a 'pre-construction data state.' 3–6 seconds: 'Sanjou' fully emerges through ultra-high-speed rectangular wipes, split panels, vertical/horizontal slide cuts, flicker frames, and glitch-synced reveals. Red corner guides and fine lines precisely indicate positions, while micro-strobe transitions and snappy position corrections create a sense of information 'locking in.' 6–9 seconds: 'Sanjou' collapses, with letters particle-izing and line-izing before shooting off-screen. From there, circular masks, rotating arcs, and concentric circle rings generate at high speed to reconstruct the space. Graphical lines and HUD overlays layer on, expressing 'energy accumulation' toward the next text emergence. 9–12 seconds: 'Nao' displays with intense zoom, snap scale, momentary contraction→re-emergence. Expanding circles, vector arcs, and high-speed pulses sync perfectly. Employ circle mat wipes, rotating masks, diagonal cuts, and line extensions extensively, with typography spacing changing dynamically. Visual rhythm builds toward its peak. 12–15 seconds: 'Nekozamurai' moves rapidly within the frame, accompanied by vibration, position jumps, skew distortion, and grid reconstruction. Layer densely with thick lines, outline boxes, modular grids, plus markers, and boundary guides. After geometric elements explosively unfold, they instantly organize, finishing with a red central title lockup on a black background. Visual style: Luxurious and minimal, extremely clean, sharp geometric shapes, digital UI-like refinement, high contrast, crisp typography, motion blur, hard edges, 8K quality, controlled glitches, broadcast-premium package texture, advanced After Effects animation language, rhythmic visual structure.

"The cat from the image is on the very back layer, repeatedly swinging a sword in time with the sound. Completely ignore the background from the reference image.

Ultra-high-speed kinetic typography (full 15 seconds), futuristic minimalist motion graphics, high-end After Effects style. Color palette consists of just three colors: black, white, and red. Extremely high-tempo staccato rhythm, precisely controlled motion, speed-filled expression, refined digital energy.

0–3 seconds:
Introduction. Fragments of 'cat samurai' are scattered as ultra-fine lines and geometric markers, repeatedly appearing and disappearing amid ultra-high-speed glitch flashes. Blackwhite backgrounds switch frame by frame, with red lines running like coordinate guides. Scan lines, noise flicker, offset text afterimages, and instantaneous split slices express a 'pre-construction data state.'

3–6 seconds:
'Sanjou' fully emerges through ultra-high-speed rectangular wipes, split panels, vertical/horizontal slide cuts, flicker frames, and glitch-synced reveals. Red corner guides and fine lines precisely indicate positions, while micro-strobe transitions and snappy position corrections create a sense of information 'locking in.'

6–9 seconds:
'Sanjou' collapses, with letters particle-izing and line-izing before shooting off-screen. From there, circular masks, rotating arcs, and concentric circle rings generate at high speed to reconstruct the space. Graphical lines and HUD overlays layer on, expressing 'energy accumulation' toward the next text emergence.

9–12 seconds:
'Nao' displays with intense zoom, snap scale, momentary contraction→re-emergence. Expanding circles, vector arcs, and high-speed pulses sync perfectly. Employ circle mat wipes, rotating masks, diagonal cuts, and line extensions extensively, with typography spacing changing dynamically. Visual rhythm builds toward its peak.

12–15 seconds:
'Nekozamurai' moves rapidly within the frame, accompanied by vibration, position jumps, skew distortion, and grid reconstruction. Layer densely with thick lines, outline boxes, modular grids, plus markers, and boundary guides. After geometric elements explosively unfold, they instantly organize, finishing with a red central title lockup on a black background.

Visual style:
Luxurious and minimal, extremely clean, sharp geometric shapes, digital UI-like refinement, high contrast, crisp typography, motion blur, hard edges, 8K quality, controlled glitches, broadcast-premium package texture, advanced After Effects animation language, rhythmic visual structure."

prompts.text: In a crimson desert of glass dunes under a dying sun, reveal the Thornmaw Monarch, a towering quadrupedal predator unlike anything ever imagined, with a body shaped like a lion crossed with a stag and a reef organism, covered in layered porcelain-white armor plates separated by glowing red seams. Its head blooms open into a circular crown of serrated bone petals, hiding multiple rotating jaws lined with luminous amber teeth. Along its back, enormous flexible spines sway like banners in the heat, each tipped with bioluminescent pollen that drifts through the air. It sprints across the dunes with explosive power, every footstep cracking the glass surface into glittering fractures. The camera tracks low as it leaps over collapsing ridges and swings its head, launching a storm of razor petals into the wind. At the climax, the Monarch charges an enormous glass colossus emerging from beneath the sand, then rears upward, opens its floral maw to full terrifying diameter, and releases a deafening shockwave roar that shatters the entire dune field into a glittering tidal wave of crystal shards. Ultra realistic texture, dramatic slow motion, heat haze, majestic and terrifying. Generated on Runway.

prompts.text: Bye bye, VFX artists? Is this really the end? Why would a studio spend $10,000 on a single VFX shot... when tools like Seedance 2.0 can generate an entire cinematic scene in minutes? That’s the uncomfortable question no one wants to answer. We’re watching a shift happen in real time. Jobs are already being reshaped. Pipelines are changing. And the speed of evolution is hard to ignore. In just 3 years, AI went from experimental... to producing complex, high-quality visuals that were unthinkable before. Now compare that to the traditional path: A VFX artist can take years—often a decade— to reach a professional level. So what happens when that gap collapses? This isn’t just about replacing artists. It’s about redefining what “creating” even means. The barrier to entry is disappearing. The tools are getting exponentially better. And the timeline is accelerating. The real question isn’t if this changes the industry— It’s how fast.

prompts.text: I subscribed to Runway on a monthly basis for the first time in a while and tried out Seedance 2.0 (Unlimited). I tested character replacement in the source video using the multi-reference feature. I'm using a live-action base video and references from an AI-generated female character. I tried the same thing with Kling 3.0 Omni as well (comparison in the replies), but under these conditions, Seedance 2.0 was significantly better. The motion reproduction is good too, but what I found particularly impressive was how it manages to keep parts other than the replacement target—backgrounds and compositions—relatively intact. That said, it does start to show a bit of that AI feel in wider shots. Still, depending on how you use it, I felt it has quite a lot of potential.

prompts.text: Made with Seedance2, reference images made with Nano Banana 2, prompt put together using GPT-5.4. 15-second CatFu meme-format duel between a tiny martial-arts cat and the robot. Dead-serious kung fu tone. The cat is a real master and also pulls a tiny gun mid-fight. The robot fights back with precise controlled counters. 0-3s: the cat opens in Qǐ Shì (起势), shifts through Xū Bù (虚步) into Gōng Bù (弓步), then snaps Tān Dǎ (摊打) and Pāk Dǎ (拍打). The robot slips the first strike, turns off-line from the second, and answers with a sharp Tuī Zhǎng (推掌) check. 3-6s: the cat drops into Mǎ Bù (马步), flows into Pú Bù (仆步), then chains Lián Huán Quán (连环拳), Héng Sǎo Tuǐ (横扫腿), and Cè Chuài Tuǐ (侧踹腿). The robot half-steps back, pivots the sweep, catches the kick line, and returns a compact Zhǒu Jī (肘击) feint into a backhand check. 6-9s: the cat coils in Xiē Bù (歇步), launches Xuán Fēng Jiǎo (旋风脚), lands, then suddenly whips out a tiny pistol with absurd confidence and fires two fast shots. The robot tilts past the first, blocks the bullet line with the back of it's hand, and flicks it aside before stepping in with Àn Zhǎng (按掌) to break the cat’s stance. 9-12s: the cat skids into Dī Pán Shì (低盘式), quick-draws again, mixes Bēng Quán (崩拳), Pī Quán (劈拳), and point-blank gunplay into one frantic combo. 12-15s: final clash. The cat vaults back into Hǔ Bù (虎步), fires one last shot, then rushes in behind it with Tān Dǎ (摊打). The robot turns the bullet aside, parries the strike, counters with a short Tuī Zhǎng (推掌), then stops the cat at the forehead with open palm. Both reset into mirrored Gōng Bù (弓步) guards. End on a serious two-shot hold.
prompts.display_text: a tiny martial-arts cat and the robot. Dead-serious kung fu tone. The cat is a real master and also pulls a tiny gun mid-fight. The robot fights back with precise controlled counters. 0-3s: the cat opens in Qǐ Shì (起势), shifts through Xū Bù (虚步) into Gōng Bù (弓步), then snaps Tān Dǎ (摊打) and Pāk Dǎ (拍打). The robot slips the first strike, turns off-line from the second, and answers with a sharp Tuī Zhǎng (推掌) check. 3-6s: the cat drops into Mǎ Bù (马步), flows into Pú Bù (仆步), then chains Lián Huán Quán (连环拳), Héng Sǎo Tuǐ (横扫腿), and Cè Chuài Tuǐ (侧踹腿). The robot half-steps back, pivots the sweep, catches the kick line, and returns a compact Zhǒu Jī (肘击) feint into a backhand check. 6-9s: the cat coils in Xiē Bù (歇步), launches Xuán Fēng Jiǎo (旋风脚), lands, then suddenly whips out a tiny pistol with absurd confidence and fires two fast shots. The robot tilts past the first, blocks the bullet line with the back of it's hand, and flicks it aside before stepping in with Àn Zhǎng (按掌) to break the cat’s stance. 9-12s: the cat skids into Dī Pán Shì (低盘式), quick-draws again, mixes Bēng Quán (崩拳), Pī Quán (劈拳), and point-blank gunplay into one frantic combo. 12-15s: final clash. End on a serious two-shot hold.

prompts.text: i made @redbull video with AI. seedance 2 prompt; Use the provided image as the first frame [Image1] . Sudden deceleration, smooth canopy inflation, stable descent. 13–15s: Glide toward a dirt landing zone near buildings. Another skydiver with a white parachute lands ahead. End with a clean landing and a ground-level high-five. Add on-screen text: “YES MATE”. Style: hyper-realistic stunt footage, cinematic action, ultra-wide POV, natural motion blur, consistent character identity, physically accurate motion, no warping, no flicker, no extra limbs, no scene cuts.

prompts.text: "A cinematic wild-animal action sequence in one continuous epic shot. Start with an ultra-wide aerial view above a stormy jungle canyon at dawn, mist pouring through the trees, waterfalls crashing into a river below, golden light cutting through dark clouds. On opposite cliffs, a massive tiger and a giant black gorilla lock eyes across the gap, both tense, powerful, and silent. The camera dives fast between them as thunder rolls. The tiger leaps first, using a fallen tree as a bridge, while the gorilla smashes through vines and rock to meet it. They collide on a narrow cliff ledge in a burst of dust, leaves, and flying stone. The camera circles tightly as they fight with speed and force, slipping near the edge while rain begins falling harder. Just as the ledge collapses beneath them, both launch apart onto separate rocks above the raging river. Final beat: they stop, turn, and look upward as an even larger shadow moves through the mist behind them." Instead of breaking it into scenes, everything is chained together: Aerial wide → fast dive → collision → fight → environmental collapse → final reveal What really matters here is motion continuity. Every action triggers the next: Camera dive leads into the clash Impact leads into close combat Environment starts breaking → forces separation Final pause sets up the last reveal That cause-and-effect flow is what keeps it from feeling random. What Makes This Work Single continuous camera movement (no hard cuts) Clear spatial geography (you always know where everything is) Momentum-driven choreography instead of static fighting Environment reacting to the action (dust, rain, collapsing ledge) Seedance 2.0 handles chaos much better when: Motion has a clear direction The camera has a defined path Big moments are spaced out (not everything happening at once) Too many disconnected actions = breaks immersion One flowing sequence = feels cinematic Why One-Shot Works So Well It forces the AI to: Maintain consistency Respect physics and spacing Keep the scene coherent over time Which is exactly where most generations usually fail. Feels like this approach is perfect for: Action scenes Creature fights Trailer-style sequences Curious if anyone else is experimenting with one-shot prompts instead of multi-cut edits. #Seedance #Seedance2 #AI #AIVIDEO #AIart️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️ #Prompt

prompts.tweet_text:
How to Create an Epic One-Shot Animal Battle in Seedance 2.0? Prompt Below! (Tiger vs Gorilla)

Tried building a full one-shot cinematic action sequence in Seedance 2.0 and no cuts, just continuous motion from start to finish.

The goal was to make it feel like a real long-take shot you’d see in a high-budget film.

Prompt:
"A cinematic wild-animal action sequence in one continuous epic shot. Start with an ultra-wide aerial view above a stormy jungle canyon at dawn, mist pouring through the trees, waterfalls crashing into a river below, golden light cutting through dark clouds. On opposite cliffs, a massive tiger and a giant black gorilla lock eyes across the gap, both tense, powerful, and silent. The camera dives fast between them as thunder rolls. The tiger leaps first, using a fallen tree as a bridge, while the gorilla smashes through vines and rock to meet it. They collide on a narrow cliff ledge in a burst of dust, leaves, and flying stone. The camera circles tightly as they fight with speed and force, slipping near the edge while rain begins falling harder. Just as the ledge collapses beneath them, both launch apart onto separate rocks above the raging river. Final beat: they stop, turn, and look upward as an even larger shadow moves through the mist behind them."

Instead of breaking it into scenes, everything is chained together:

Aerial wide → fast dive → collision → fight → environmental collapse → final reveal

What really matters here is motion continuity.

Every action triggers the next:

Camera dive leads into the clash

Impact leads into close combat

Environment starts breaking → forces separation

Final pause sets up the last reveal

That cause-and-effect flow is what keeps it from feeling random.

What Makes This Work

Single continuous camera movement (no hard cuts)

Clear spatial geography (you always know where everything is)

Momentum-driven choreography instead of static fighting

Environment reacting to the action (dust, rain, collapsing ledge)

Seedance 2.0 handles chaos much better when:

Motion has a clear direction

The camera has a defined path

Big moments are spaced out (not everything happening at once)

Too many disconnected actions = breaks immersion
One flowing sequence = feels cinematic

Why One-Shot Works So Well
It forces the AI to:

Maintain consistency

Respect physics and spacing

Keep the scene coherent over time

Which is exactly where most generations usually fail.

Feels like this approach is perfect for:

Action scenes

Creature fights

Trailer-style sequences

Curious if anyone else is experimenting with one-shot prompts instead of multi-cut edits.
#Seedance #Seedance2 #AI #AIVIDEO #AIart️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️️ #Prompt

prompts.text: Seedance V2 Now the prompt is out you can change the character's the food and the location, have fun realistic live-action, single continuous shot, full first-person POV, no cuts, no angle changes, no scene transitions, no montage, no flash cuts, no music, no subtitles. Only two characters: my unseen first-person POV and @ Image1 sitting across from me. Natural ambient sound only: light restaurant noise, tableware, distant water, faint voices, chewing, spitting, rinsing, my laughter. Setting: Outdoor dining table beside West Lake, Hangzhou, near the water, calm daylight or soft late afternoon light. Real smartphone-video realism, grounded textures, realistic food, realistic body and hand motion, accurate chopstick use, accurate mouth movement, accurate eye focus. The lake stays visible to one side of frame. Character: @ Image1 is the only visible person. She can switch seamlessly between slightly fierce-cute and soft adorable. Lip sync, voice, expressions, and body language stay fully synchronized. Emotional arc: cheerful and relaxed while eating, then shocked disgust, then angry embarrassment. Table: Exactly 7 dishes on the table. One dish is the West Lake vinegar fish shown in @ Image2. @ Image1 is happily eating rice and the other dishes, but not the fish at first. The table feels full but readable. The fish dish remains clearly visible before I offer it to her. Camera: Entire video is from my seated first-person view across the table. My hand and chopsticks may enter frame naturally. Slight handheld micro-shake from breathing and laughter only, never chaotic. No third-person shots, no reverse shots, no overhead shots, no cutaways. Continuous believable framing centered on @ Image1 with part of the lake visible. Action: @ Image1 sits opposite me, happily eating from her bowl, enjoying rice and other dishes except the West Lake vinegar fish. She looks bright, playful, relaxed, cute. From first-person POV, I pick up one bite of the fish with chopsticks and slowly extend it toward her. She notices it immediately, trusts me, leans forward, opens her mouth happily, and makes a cute “aam” sound as she bites the fish. I gently pull the chopsticks and my hand back out of frame. She chews happily for about one second, expecting it to taste good. Then the taste hits. Her expression changes instantly into exaggerated but believable disgust. Her face shifts into a comedic purple-disgust reaction while staying within realistic live-action style. She quickly turns toward the lake and spits it out with repeated “ptui, ptui, ptui” sounds. The motion is fast, fluid, and continuous from chewing to rejection. The moment she starts spitting, I begin laughing in first-person POV and keep laughing through the whole reaction. My laughter sounds genuine, amused, and uncontrollable, coming from behind the camera. The camera shakes slightly from laughing. After spitting, @ Image1 sticks out her tongue in disgust, immediately grabs the water on the table, and rinses her mouth quickly. She looks offended, embarrassed, irritated, but still vivid and cute. After rinsing, she snaps into angry frustration, grabs the entire fish dish from the table, and throws it into the nearby lake in one continuous motion. Ending: She finishes the throw angrily, still facing the lake, clearly annoyed and feeling betrayed, while I remain in first-person POV laughing. Keep the action continuous and clean. No extra characters enter frame.

She finishes the throw angrily, still facing the lake, clearly annoyed and feeling betrayed, while I remain in first-person POV laughing. Keep the action continuous and clean. No extra characters enter frame.

prompts.author: @ShamiWeb3
prompts.text: Create a 4-second ultra-cute vertical animated character video. A tiny fluffy purple puppy-like creature with soft fur, a round face, big glossy expressive eyes, a small black nose, short floppy ears, and adorable baby proportions. The style is polished soft 3D animation, very cute, smooth fur, pastel purple background, soft studio lighting. The character walks forward slowly with natural body bounce and soft paw movement, first looking slightly away with an innocent expression. Then it turns its head and upper body toward the camera, gives a surprised but cheeky look, and raises one hand to show one middle finger clearly. It then turns forward again for a brief beat as if continuing to walk, then quickly looks back at the camera with a more mischievous expression and raises both hands, showing two middle fingers clearly at the same time. End on the double gesture for a brief moment so it is readable. Focus on smooth body plasticity, head turn, shoulder turn, eye movement, hand gesture clarity, and cute facial expression. The eyes must be large, shiny, and very expressive. The fur must look soft and detailed. The motion must be funny, cute, and playful, not aggressive. No text on screen. No captions. No overlays. Visual style: ultra-cute polished 3D character animation, soft pastel lighting, clean background, meme-ready, adorable but sassy. No realism, no horror, no ugly face, no distorted eyes, no extra fingers, no extra limbs, no blurry motion, no low-detail fur, no dark background, no aggressive tone, no human body proportions.
